events.title: To Have or To Be? by Erich Fromm (week 1)
events.description: *To Have or To Be?* is one of the seminal books of the second half of the 20th century. His thesis is that two modes of existence struggle for the spirit of humankind: the having mode, which concentrates on material possessions, power, and aggression, and is the basis of the universal evils of greed, envy, and violence; and the being mode, which is based on love, the pleasure of sharing, and in productive activity. events.title: Spiritually Attacked: Becoming the Calm in the Storm (online)
events.description:
There are moments in life when it feels like everything is coming at you at once.

Heavy emotions. Strange tension. Sudden conflict. Mental noise. Exhaustion. Fear that does not seem to belong to the moment. Sometimes people call it stress. Sometimes they call it burnout. And sometimes, deeper down, it feels spiritual.

This session is for those moments.

In this Shurch gathering, we will explore what people mean when they say they feel “spiritually attacked” and how to respond without collapsin

events.title: How to Prevent Misunderstandings
events.description:
Preventing Misunderstandings

How often have you said or thought “why don’t they understand me?”
As we all know, misunderstandings are very frustrating and can lead to breakdowns in relationships. Whether the relationship is with a significant other, family member, friend or work colleague, relationships are the basis of life.
Join us for a lively discussion on how to prevent misunderstandings.
Discover the three factors that lead to true understanding.

events.title: Word and Object by Quine Week 1
events.description: How does language come to have meaning and can we trust natural language to be a reliable vehicle for truth? For many in the early analytic tradition, the answer to the meaning of language is intimately related to a correspondence theory of truth where one needs to painstakingly tie words to their reference objects in the world. For these philosophers, logic is the correct tool in order to tame natural language and train it to be a reliable vehicle for truth. For Quine (1908-2000), this is simpl

events.title: NYC Code and Coffee 85 at Datadog HQ
events.description:
[Code and Coffee](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ny-code-coffee/) is an inclusive, informal co-working session. People of all skill levels attend, and we love it that way. Many people (optionally) bring projects to work on, and many other people (optionally) socialize the entire time. It's entirely up to you!

**The Intro Circle**
**Near the beginning of the event (2:30 pm), we do a standup in a circle:**

* We first share organizer announcements, updates, and logistics

events.title: Word and Object by Quine Week 4
events.description: How does language come to have meaning and can we trust natural language to be a reliable vehicle for truth? For many in the early analytic tradition, the answer to the meaning of language is intimately related to a correspondence theory of truth where one needs to painstakingly tie words to their reference objects in the world.
